question: Will Microsoft dip to $353 in April?

events.title: What will S&P 500 (SPX) hit in March?

question: Will Zhejiang Zhiye FC win on 2026-04-05?
description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 5, 2026
If Zhejiang Zhiye FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Zhejiang Zhiye FC vs. Chongqing Tonglianglong FC

events.title: What will NYSE Composite (NYA) hit in March?
events.description: What will NYSE Composite (NYA) hit in March?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket tilts toward modest NYSE Composite gains in March, with implied probabilities favoring levels above 18,000 amid expectations of a Federal Reserve pause on rate hikes. The index recently closed near 17,950, buoyed by cooling inflation—January CPI at 3.1% year-over-year—and robust corporate earnings, though high valuations (S&P 500 P/E near 21x) cap upside. Key catalysts include March 12 CPI data, March 14 PPI release, and the FOMC meeting on March 19-20, where dot plot updates could affirm three 2024 rate cuts, boosting risk assets. Geopolitical tensions and Q1 earnings kicks off March 11 pose downside risks, underscoring market-implied odds' sensitivity to macro data.

events.title: What will Nikkei 225 (NIK) hit in March?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket tilts toward the Nikkei 225 sustaining above 39,000 by March's end, with implied probabilities around 65% for levels exceeding recent peaks near 40,000, fueled by a weakening yen at 151 USD/JPY that amplifies exporter profits like Toyota and Sony. events.title: What will DAX (DAX) hit in March?
events.description: What will DAX (DAX) hit in March?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket implies a 55% probability for the DAX to close March above 19,000, reflecting optimism from expected ECB rate cuts amid eurozone inflation easing to 2.4% in January. The index, currently hovering near 19,200 after a 5% YTD rally fueled by strong German exports and AI-driven gains in SAP and Siemens, faces headwinds from persistent manufacturing contraction (PMI at 43.1) and U.S. tariff risks. Key catalysts include the ECB's March 6 meeting, where 25bps easing is fully priced, and February 28 German GDP data—watch for sub-0.5% contraction triggering bearish shifts below 18,500 support. Market-implied odds underscore real-money bets on resilient EU growth versus global slowdown fears.

question: Will Elon Musk post 300-319 tweets from March 17 to March 24, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from March 17 12:00 PM ET to March 24, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.
